function_v0.doc: The Blend pool address (read-only view, handy for the frontend / dashboard).
function_v0.name: pool

function_v0.doc: Withdraw `shares` worth of underlying from Blend and send it to `to`. Returns underlying out.
function_v0.name: redeem

function_v0.doc: Pull `amount` USDC from `from` (the wrapper) and supply it to Blend. Returns shares minted.
function_v0.name: deposit

function_v0.doc: Human-readable semver of the source build (informational; for verifiable identity use\n[`Self::code_hash`]).
function_v0.name: version

function_v0.doc: The live deployed WASM hash (32-byte SHA-256) of the running code \xe2\x80\x94 reflects the current\nbuild even across upgrades.
function_v0.name: code_hash

function_v0.doc:
One-shot, admin-gated initialization.

* `admin` \xe2\x80\x94 operational admin (pause/upgrade path; cannot move user funds).
* `wrapper` \xe2\x80\x94 the Spield wrapper; the sole authorized caller of deposit/redeem.
* `pool` \xe2\x80\x94 the Blend pool contract.
* `underlying` \xe2\x80\x94 the asset SAC (USDC) to supply.
* `max_apr_bps` \xe2\x80\x94 max allowed **annual** `b_rate` growth, in basis points (the sanity bound,
pro-rated by elapsed time on each read). Set generously above Blend's real max borrow APR
(e.g. `30_000` = 300%) so honest reads always pass while a wild read is still caught.
function_v0.name: initialize

function_v0.doc: The current `b_rate` sanity bound: `(last_rate, last_ts, max_apr_bps)`. View for monitoring \xe2\x80\x94\nlets ops watch the last observed rate/time and the configured annual-growth ceiling.
function_v0.name: rate_bound

function_v0.doc: Accept a pending admin proposal (step 2 of 2). Must be called by the proposed admin.
function_v0.name: accept_admin

function_v0.doc: Live Blend `b_rate` (SCALAR_12), with the sanity bound applied and `last_rate` advanced.
function_v0.name: current_rate

function_v0.doc: Total shares this strategy holds in Blend (the wrapper's whole position).
function_v0.name: total_shares

function_v0.doc: **Atomic deploy-time constructor (no deploy\xe2\x86\x92init front-run).** Binds `admin` the moment the\nstrategy exists; the remaining [`Self::initialize`] is then gated to this admin.
function_v0.name: __constructor

function_v0.doc: Propose a new admin (step 1 of 2). Current admin authorizes; the proposed admin must then\ncall `accept_admin` to take control.
function_v0.name: propose_admin

function_v0.doc: Underlying value of `shares` at the live rate.
function_v0.name: position_value

function_v0.doc:
Widen (or tighten) the max **annual** `b_rate` growth, in basis points (the bound is then
pro-rated by elapsed time on each read \xe2\x80\x94 see `check_rate_bound_timed`). Admin only.

This is the safety valve for the soft-brick risk: if Blend's real `b_rate` ever rises faster
than the configured annual ceiling, **every** `current_rate` read would otherwise panic and
freeze the whole protocol (no claims/redeems/mints/solvency reads). The admin can raise the
ceiling to unstick it without a redeploy. `last_rate`/`last_ts` are preserved.

Intentionally *immediate* (not timelocked): it is a liveness safety valve that can only widen
tolerance on an already-trusted, monotonic Blend rate \xe2\x80\x94 it can never mint value or move funds.
Raising it does not bypass any solvency check; the wrapper still asserts `backing >= principal`
against Blend's *real* position on every mutation.
function_v0.name: set_max_apr_bps

function_v0.doc: Schedule a contract upgrade to `wasm_hash`, applyable after the timelock. Returns the `eta`.
function_v0.name: schedule_upgrade

function_v0.doc: Withdraw exactly `amount` underlying from Blend and send it to `to`. Returns shares burned.
function_v0.name: redeem_underlying

function_v0.doc: Cancel a pending admin proposal. Current admin only.
function_v0.name: cancel_admin_transfer

event_v0.doc: A scheduled upgrade was applied \xe2\x80\x94 the contract now runs `wasm_hash`.
event_v0.name: Upgraded

event_v0.doc: A proposed admin accepted the role (two-step rotation, step 2). `new_admin` is now in control.
event_v0.name: AdminChanged

event_v0.doc: A new admin was proposed (two-step rotation, step 1).
event_v0.name: AdminProposed

fields.doc: Earliest unix-second timestamp at which `apply_upgrade` may run (`scheduled_at + timelock`).
fields.name: eta

fields.doc: The WASM hash the contract will be upgraded to.
fields.name: wasm_hash

event_v0.doc: The upgrade timelock delay was changed.
event_v0.name: TimelockChanged

event_v0.doc: A scheduled upgrade was cancelled before it was applied.
event_v0.name: UpgradeCancelled

event_v0.doc: An upgrade was scheduled; it may be applied at/after `eta`.
event_v0.name: UpgradeScheduled

event_v0.doc: A pending admin proposal was cancelled by the current admin.
event_v0.name: AdminTransferCancelled

udt_struct_v0.doc:
A single deposit's accounting record \xe2\x80\x94 the unit that makes Spield's yield math correct.

**Per-position, never overwritten** (fixes SCF #4): every `mint` creates a *new* `Position`
with its own `entry_rate`, so topping up never clobbers an earlier tranche's entry point.

**`settled_rate` travels with the YT** (fixes SCF #5/#6): yield is always measured from
`settled_rate`, which starts at `entry_rate` and is bumped to the current rate on every
`claim_yield` *without burning YT*. A position transferred to a new owner carries its
`settled_rate`, so the buyer can only ever claim yield accrued *after* they held it.
udt_struct_v0.name: Position

fields.doc: Blend `b_rate` (SCALAR_12) at the moment this position was minted. Immutable.
fields.name: entry_rate

fields.doc: False once the position is fully redeemed/closed (all PT + YT gone).
fields.name: open

fields.doc: Current owner of this position (and of the PT + YT it represents).
fields.name: owner

fields.doc: Underlying principal deposited for this position, in the underlying's decimals (USDC).\nEqual to the PT amount still outstanding and the YT amount still outstanding (1:1:1 at\nmint; PT and YT are only reduced by `redeem_pt` / `combine_and_redeem`).
fields.name: principal

fields.doc: PT still held in this position. Burned on `redeem_pt` (after maturity) and on `combine`.
fields.name: pt_amount

fields.doc: Blend `b_rate` (SCALAR_12) up to which yield has already been settled/paid. Starts equal\nto `entry_rate`; advanced to `current_rate` on each claim. Yield owed is measured from\nhere, so the same YT can be claimed across many epochs.
fields.name: settled_rate

fields.doc: Blend bToken shares this position is backed by (its slice of the wrapper's total Blend\nposition). `principal = shares * entry_rate / SCALAR_12` at mint.
fields.name: shares

fields.doc: YT still held in this position. **Never burned by `claim_yield`** \xe2\x80\x94 only by `combine`.
fields.name: yt_amount

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Defence-in-depth bound on `b_rate` reads, stored in the strategy adapter's config.

The bound is **time-aware**: `b_rate` may rise by at most `max_apr_bps` per year, pro-rated by
the seconds elapsed since `last_ts`. This makes the check independent of how often the strategy
is read (a long-untouched position no longer false-trips), so only `max_apr_bps` \xe2\x80\x94 calibrated
against Blend's real max borrow APR \xe2\x80\x94 needs tuning. See [`crate::math::check_rate_bound_timed`].
udt_struct_v0.name: RateBound

fields.doc: Last `b_rate` the strategy observed (SCALAR_12). Used to enforce monotonicity + the ceiling.
fields.name: last_rate

fields.doc: Unix-second timestamp at which `last_rate` was observed. The elapsed time since this is what\nthe allowed increase is pro-rated by. `0` = no observation yet (first read bypasses the cap).
fields.name: last_ts

fields.doc: Max allowed **annual** `b_rate` growth, in basis points (e.g. `30_000` = 300% APR). Set\ngenerously above Blend's real max borrow APR so honest reads always pass.
fields.name: max_apr_bps

udt_struct_v0.doc: Read-only snapshot of the Fixed-Rate Vault's health, for the frontend / solvency dashboard.\nThe vault is solvent iff `pt_inventory >= total_liability` (it holds enough PT to honor every\noutstanding receipt at par).
udt_struct_v0.name: VaultStats

fields.doc: `pt_inventory - total_liability`: spare PT available to back new coupons (the headroom\nthat lets the vault quote a fixed rate). Negative would mean insolvency (never allowed).
fields.name: coupon_capacity

fields.doc: The vault's maturity (unix seconds).
fields.name: maturity

fields.doc: PT the vault currently holds (its bond inventory). Each unit redeems 1:1 at maturity.
fields.name: pt_inventory

fields.doc: The current fixed APR the vault quotes, in basis points.
fields.name: rate_bps

fields.doc: Sum of `payout` across all open receipts \xe2\x80\x94 the vault's total obligation at maturity.
fields.name: total_liability

fields.doc: YT the vault currently holds (the variable leg whose yield funds future coupons).
fields.name: yt_inventory

udt_struct_v0.doc:
A single Fixed-Rate Vault deposit (plan \xc2\xa711.2 / \xc2\xa77.5 \xe2\x80\x94 the flagship "lock X% fixed" product).

PT-passthrough model: the user deposits `principal` USDC and is promised exactly `payout`
USDC at maturity (`payout = principal + coupon`, the coupon being the fixed return). The
vault backs every receipt with **PT it actually holds** (each PT redeems 1:1 at maturity),
so the fixed rate is solvent by construction \xe2\x80\x94 the same rigor as the wrapper's invariant.
There is no per-user yield accounting here: the user's outcome is fixed and known at deposit.
udt_struct_v0.name: FixedReceipt

fields.doc: Unix seconds at which `payout` becomes redeemable (the vault's maturity).
fields.name: maturity

fields.doc: False once redeemed.
fields.name: open

fields.doc: Owner of this receipt (the only account that may redeem it).
fields.name: owner

fields.doc: USDC the user is guaranteed at maturity = principal + fixed coupon. Backed by PT the\nvault holds 1:1, so it is always redeemable.
fields.name: payout

fields.doc: USDC principal the user deposited.
fields.name: principal

fields.doc: The fixed APR quoted for this receipt, in basis points (for display / events only \xe2\x80\x94 the\neconomically binding figure is `payout`).
fields.name: rate_bps

udt_struct_v0.doc: A read-only snapshot returned to the frontend / solvency dashboard: the live, Blend-backed\nvalue of a position, split into its principal and currently-claimable yield.
udt_struct_v0.name: PositionValue

fields.doc: Yield claimable right now: `yt_amount * (current_rate - settled_rate) / SCALAR_12`.
fields.name: claimable_yield

udt_error_enum_v0.doc: Spield error codes, shared across contracts. Numbering leaves gaps so each contract's\ndomain stays grouped: 1\xe2\x80\x9319 generic/lifecycle, 20\xe2\x80\x9339 wrapper accounting, 40\xe2\x80\x9359 strategy.
udt_error_enum_v0.name: Error

cases.doc: `initialize` called a second time (SCF #7: one-shot init guard).
cases.name: AlreadyInitialized

cases.doc: A function needing prior `initialize` was called first.
cases.name: NotInitialized

cases.doc: Caller is not the configured admin.
cases.name: NotAuthorized

cases.doc: Contract is paused by the circuit breaker; mutating calls are halted.
cases.name: Paused

cases.doc: A supplied amount was zero or negative where a positive value is required.
cases.name: InvalidAmount

cases.doc: Arithmetic overflowed (should be unreachable with i128 + overflow-checks, but asserted).
cases.name: MathOverflow

cases.doc: `accept_admin` / `cancel_admin_transfer` called with no admin proposal pending.
cases.name: NoPendingAdmin

cases.doc: `apply_upgrade` / `cancel_upgrade` called with no upgrade scheduled.
cases.name: NoPendingUpgrade

cases.doc: `apply_upgrade` called before the scheduled upgrade's timelock `eta`.
cases.name: TimelockNotElapsed

cases.doc: `set_timelock` value outside the allowed `[MIN_TIMELOCK_SECS, MAX_TIMELOCK_SECS]` range.
cases.name: TimelockOutOfBounds

cases.doc: The underlying token's `decimals()` is not the value the fixed-point math is calibrated for\n(7 on Stellar USDC). Asserted at init rather than assumed.
cases.name: UnexpectedDecimals

cases.doc: The referenced position id does not exist.
cases.name: PositionNotFound

cases.doc: Caller does not own the referenced position.
cases.name: NotPositionOwner

cases.doc: `redeem_pt` called before `maturity`.
cases.name: NotMatured

cases.doc: Tried to redeem/split more PT or YT than the position holds.
cases.name: InsufficientBalance

cases.doc: The solvency invariant would be violated by this operation \xe2\x80\x94 refuse it.\n(SCF #3: the vault can never promise value Blend hasn't actually accrued.)
cases.name: SolvencyViolation

cases.doc: The position has already been fully redeemed/closed.
cases.name: PositionClosed

cases.doc: Blend returned a `bRate` outside the configured sanity bound (defence-in-depth).
cases.name: RateOutOfBounds

cases.doc: Blend reported no supplied position for the asset we expected to hold.
cases.name: NoStrategyPosition

cases.doc: Withdrawal from Blend returned less underlying than required (liquidity edge case).
cases.name: WithdrawShortfall

cases.doc: The referenced fixed-rate receipt id does not exist.
cases.name: ReceiptNotFound

cases.doc: Caller does not own the referenced receipt.
cases.name: NotReceiptOwner

cases.doc: `redeem` called before the vault's maturity.
cases.name: VaultNotMatured

cases.doc: The receipt has already been redeemed.
cases.name: ReceiptClosed

cases.doc: The vault lacks enough spare PT (coupon capacity) to back the coupon for this deposit.\nRefusing keeps the vault solvent by construction (the SCF #3 bar): a fixed coupon is only\never promised when the vault already holds the PT to honor it.
cases.name: InsufficientCapacity

cases.doc: The quoted fixed rate is out of the allowed range (e.g. above the admin-set ceiling).
cases.name: RateNotAllowed

cases.doc: Deposit rejected because the market has already matured (no time left to earn the coupon,\nand PT can no longer be accumulated). Vault maturity is inherited from the wrapper.
cases.name: VaultExpired

cases.doc: A swap/liquidity op would empty a pool reserve (proportion must stay strictly in (0,1)).
cases.name: InsufficientLiquidity

cases.doc: The realized output was below the caller's `min_out` slippage guard \xe2\x80\x94 reverted.
cases.name: SlippageExceeded

cases.doc: Trading attempted at/after the market's maturity (after maturity PT just redeems 1:1).
cases.name: MarketExpired

cases.doc: `remove_liquidity` for more LP shares than the caller holds (or than exist).
cases.name: InsufficientShares

cases.doc: `add_liquidity` deposit ratio doesn't match the pool's current reserves (would mis-price\nexisting LPs). The caller must supply PT and USDC in the pool's current proportion.
cases.name: ImbalancedLiquidity

cases.doc: The swap fee (bps) exceeds the on-chain ceiling set at init (a guardrail).
cases.name: FeeNotAllowed
